cases.title: Nexus Telecom vs. Horizon AI Software Intellectual Property Dispute
cases.description: Patent infringement action under Section 64 of the Patents Act, 1970 against Horizon Inc. regarding neural network rendering patents. Horizon claims prior art from 2023. Our engineering reports indicate clear structural derivative code in Horizon's production models. Seeking permanent injunction and damages.
cases.oppositionLawyer: Senior Advocate Harish Salve, Shardul Amarchand Mangaldas

cases.title: Apex Commercial Lease Breach & Subtenant Eviction
cases.description: Lease breach action under Section 108 of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 for unauthorized sub-letting of commercial premises at Bandra West. The sub-tenant is running a hazardous material storage service in breach of Section 12-C of the prime lease deed. Demanding immediate cure or vacant possession.

cases.title: SEBI Compliance Advisory & NCLT Reorganization

cases.description: Advising AG Corporate Holdings on regulatory compliance following SEBI's updated Insider Trading regulations. Drafting customized fiduciary duty compliance protocols under Companies Act, 2013 and reorganizing board audit committee bylaws to safeguard stakeholder representation.

cases.title: State of Maharashtra v. Rajesh Mehta & Ors.
cases.description: Defense of director Rajesh Mehta on charges of felony insider trading and criminal breach of trust under the Indian Penal Code / Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), relative to Apex stock price changes during late 2025. Resulted in a negotiated compounding of offense and administrative penalty. Closed successfully with no imprisonment.

cachedStatutorySections.chapterName: Chapter VIII - General and Special Practice Provisions
cachedStatutorySections.title: Detailed statutory guidelines on Property practice code
cachedStatutorySections.description: This detailed statutory section regulates general legal procedures, rights, and standard liabilities relative to Property legal disputes in India. Sub-section (1) establishes that every affected entity or citizen has the absolute right to direct representation. Sub-section (2) provides that any violations entail legal compensation or penalties specified by rules.
cachedStatutorySections.explanationsProvisos: Provided that nothing in this clause shall apply to actions taken in good faith or as authorized by public administrators of the Indian Union. Explanation: For the purpose of this section, regulatory compliance is governed under central rules.
cachedStatutorySections.amendmentsNotifications: Amended by the Consolidated Legal Amendments Act, 2023 with effect from August 15, 2023, expanding regulatory fines. Notification No. REG-441/2026: Directs that digital filings are fully verified legally.

cachedCaseLaws.caseName: Consolidated Solutions Corp. v. Union of India and Others
cachedCaseLaws.summary: This landmark judicial decision from the Supreme Court of India addresses the deep procedural disputes on Property. The petitioning party Consolidated Solutions Corp. entered appeals challenging executive show-cause notices which were issued without traditional opportunity. The Court, led by Justice D.Y. Chandrachud, examined the doctrine of constitutional reasonableness, holding that failure to serve standard notice violates Article 14 rules. The appeal was allowed, and all impugned administrative demands were set aside.
keyPrinciples: Procedural reasonableness is an essential safeguard of administrative actions under Property practice.
Arbitrary unilateral declarations violate constitutional guarantees of equity and fairness.
Provisions of The Transfer of Property Act, 1882 must be interpreted harmoniously with civil procedure rules.
applicableSections: The Transfer of Property Act, 1882 - Section 174
relatedPrecedents: Shreya Singhal v. Union of India (2015)
Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala (1973)
cachedCaseLaws.recentUpdate: Adopted as a major precedent by High Courts nationwide in subsequent commercial matters.
